standard_description: Use place value understanding to round decimals to any place.
subject: Math

problems.text:
Problem 1
Find each quotient. Be prepared to explain your reasoning.
8 ÷ 2 = _________
0.8 ÷ 2 = _________
0.08 ÷ 2 = _________
What is the relationship between the quotients in Parts (i) and (ii) above? Between Parts (i) and (iii)? .
Problem 2
Compute.
0.06 ÷ 3 = ___________
0.24 ÷ 4 = ___________
Problem 3
Estimate each of the following quotients.
7.92 ÷ 4
3.6 ÷ 5
0.52 ÷ 6

Task
A number $n$ is shown on the number line.
###IMAGE0###
1. The tick marks are evenly spaced. Label them. 2. What is $n$ rounded to the nearest hundredth? 3. What is $n$ rounded to the nearest tenth?

Problem 1
What is the value of
$$0.24 \div 3$$
?
Problem 2
Estimate
$$3.46 \div 4$$
.

Problem 1
What is the value of
$$1.4 \div 0.02$$
?
Problem 2
Estimate
$$4.92 \div 0.78$$
.

Problem 1
Choose from the options below to fill in the blanks with an operation symbol and a number to make a true statement.
###IMAGE0###
Problem 2
Compute 4 × 0.06.
Problem 3
Which value is closest to the product of 7 × 0.39?

Problem 1
a.   Solve.
3 eighths – 1 fourth = ___________
3 fifths – 1 half = ___________
3 tenths – 1 hundredth = ___________
b.   What do you notice about Part (a) above? What do you wonder?
Problem 2
a.   Estimate the following differences.
0.8 – 0.2
0.94 – 0.6
2 – 0.34
73.73 – 41.123
b.   Solve for the actual differences in Part (a) above. Are your answers reasonable? Why or why not?
Problem 3
The decimal points have been erased from the minuend and subtrahend on the left-hand side of each equation below. Place a decimal point in each value on the left-hand side to make the equation true.
a.   1272 – 59 = 6.82
b.   21 – 63 = 1.47

Problem 1
Myra, Leo, and Dagon used different methods to solve 9 ÷ 0.3. All three computations were correct.
###TABLE0###
Which method makes the most sense to you? Why?
What might Myra do to compute 4.5 ÷ 0.5? What might Leo do? What might Dagon do? Will the three methods result in the same quotient?
Problem 2
Compute.
6 ÷ 0.02
0.54 ÷ 0.06
0.8 ÷ 0.04
5.6 ÷ 0.07
Problem 3
Estimate the following quotients. Determine whether the actual quotient will be greater or less than the estimated quotient, or whether it’s difficult to determine.
29.44 ÷ 0.86
4 ÷ 0.51
1.43 ÷ 0.72
0.31 ÷ 0.06
7.05 ÷ 0.08

Problem 1
Which of the following values would
not
round to 7.4 when rounded to the nearest tenth?
Problem 2
Which two statements about rounding decimals are correct?

Problem 1
a.   The number 8.263 lies between 8 and 9 on the number line. Label all the other tick marks between 8 and 9 and then determine if 8.263 is closer to 8 or 9 on the number line.
###IMAGE0###
b.   Which tenth is 8.263 nearest to on the number line? Determine what values the two outermost spots on the number line below should be to help you determine which tenth 8.263 is closest to. Then plot 8.263 to prove your answer.
###IMAGE1###
c.   Which hundredth is 8.263 nearest to on the number line? Determine what values the two outermost spots on the number line below should be to help you determine which hundredth 8.263 is closest to. Then plot 8.263 to prove your answer.
###IMAGE2###
Problem 2
Round each of the following values to the places mentioned. Record your answer with the “
$$\approx$$
" symbol.
a.   49.67 to the nearest whole and tenth
b.   9.935 to the nearest tenth and hundredth
c.   4.3816 to the nearest whole, tenth, hundredth, and thousandth
Problem 3
###IMAGE3###
a.   What do you notice about this image? What do you wonder?
b.   The amount of sugar in a serving of this gum is a decimal number with two digits to the right of the decimal point. What is the greatest possible amount of sugar a serving of this gum can have?
c.   Use your reasoning from Part (b) above to determine the greatest and the least value (to the thousandths place) that could round to 0.27.

Problem 1
a.   Helen, Ramiro, and Jackie all have square garden plots in their community garden.
Helen plants 0.1 of her garden with flowers. Of those flowers, 0.1 of them are tulips. How much of Helen’s garden is tulips?
Ramiro plants 0.3 of his garden with flowers. Of those flowers, 0.1 of them are tulips. How much of Ramiro’s garden is tulips?
Jackie plants 0.3 of her garden with flowers. Of those flowers, 0.2 of them are tulips. How much of Jackie’s garden is tulips?
b.   Write equations to represent each problem in Part (a) above. What do you notice? What do you wonder?
Problem 2
a.   Select all expressions that are equivalent to
$$0.4 \times 0.02$$
.
$$4 \times 0.1 \times 2 \times 0.01$$
$$4 \times 0.1 \times 2 \times 0.1$$
$$4 \times \frac{1}{10} \times 2 \times \frac{1}{100}$$
$$4 \times \frac{1}{100} \times 2 \times \frac{1}{1,000}$$
$$4 \times 2 \times 0.01 \times 0.01$$
$$4 \times 2 \times \frac{1}{10} \times \frac{1}{100}$$
$$\frac{4}{10} \times \frac{2}{100}$$
b.   Find the value of
$$0.4 \times 0.02$$
. Show your reasoning.
Problem 3
Find the value of each product below. Show or explain your work.
a.   0.09 × 0.3 = _________
b.   0.7 × 0.6 = _________
c.   0.8 × 0.05 = _________
Problem 4
Estimate the following products. Determine whether the actual product will be greater or less than the estimated product or whether it’s difficult to determine.
a.   6.23 × 8.4
b.   5.4 × 0.79
c.   0.34 × 0.56
d.   0.21 × 0.03

Problem 1
a.   Find each product. Be prepared to explain your reasoning.
$$4 \times 0.1 =$$
_________
$$4 \times 0.01 =$$
_________
b.   What is the relationship between the 4 and the product in Parts (i) and (ii) above?
Problem 2
a.   Find each product. Be prepared to explain your reasoning.
$$4 \times 2 =$$
_________
$$4 \times 0.2 =$$
_________
$$4 \times 0.02 =$$
_________
b.   What is the relationship between the products in Parts (i) and (ii) above? Between Parts (i) and (iii)?
Problem 3
Compute.
a.
$$3 \times 0.03$$
b.
$$7 \times 0.8$$
c.
$$6 \times 0.05$$
Problem 4
Estimate the following products.
a.   4 × 8.24
b.   8 × 0.95
c.   6 × 0.68
d.   5 × 0.17

Problem 1
Round each of the following to the nearest whole.
a.   8.546
$${{\approx}}$$
___________
b.   11.499
$${{\approx}}$$
___________
Problem 2
Round the following to the nearest hundred, ten, and one.
149.075

Problem 1
Is 7.6 closer to 7 or closer to 8? Plot 7 and 8 on the two outermost spots on the number line below. Then plot 7.6 to prove your answer.
###IMAGE0###
Problem 2
Round each of the following numbers to the nearest hundred, ten, and whole. Record your answer with the “
$$\approx$$
” symbol
a.   99.248
b.   314.5
c.   24.93

Problem 1
a.   Solve.
3 eighths + 1 fourth = ___________
3 fifths + 1 half = ___________
3 tenths + 1 hundredth = ___________
b.   What do you notice about Part (a) above? What do you wonder?
Problem 2
a.   Estimate the following sums.
0.3 + 0.5
0.64 + 0.7
0.2 + 15
4.99 + 56.123 + 123.01 + 3.7
b.   Solve for the actual sums in Part (a) above. Are your answers reasonable? Why or why not?
Problem 3
The decimal points have been erased from the addends on the left-hand side of each equation below. Place a decimal point in each addend to make the equation true.
a.   3419 + 267 = 60.89
b.   5 + 361 + 47 + 20 = 10.36

Problem 1
Which equation shows a correct strategy and product for the expression shown?
$$0.04 \times 0.8$$
Problem 2
Estimate
$$0.5 \times 0.63$$
.

Hundredths

hundredth? 3. What is  rounded to the nearest tenth?
